lesson.title: Kalman Filter for Pairs Trading
lesson.body: The Kalman filter can estimate a dynamic hedge ratio β_t that adapts over time. Treat y_t = β_t·x_t + ε_t with β_t evolving as a random walk: β_t = β_{t-1} + δ_t. Each new observation updates the hedge ratio estimate and its uncertainty. The resulting spread y_t − β̂_t·x_t is more stationary than one using a static β.

rooms.question: Why use a Kalman filter hedge ratio instead of static OLS for pairs trading?
answers: It adapts to regime changes in the relationship between the two assets
It produces a higher Sharpe ratio by definition
It requires less historical data
It avoids the need for cointegration testing
rooms.explanation: A dynamic hedge ratio via Kalman filter tracks structural changes (regime shifts, fundamental changes) that a static OLS estimate would miss.

rooms.question: In the Kalman filter pairs trading model, the state x_t represents:
answers: The dynamic hedge ratio β_t
The stock price
The spread itself
The position size
rooms.explanation: We model: y_t = β_t·x_t + ε_t with β_t as the hidden state. The Kalman filter estimates β_t recursively from new price observations.

rooms.question: The Kalman filter transition variance δ² (variance of β_t − β_{t-1}) controls:
answers: How quickly the hedge ratio can change (higher δ² = more flexible β_t)
The trading signal threshold
The position holding period
The correlation between assets
rooms.explanation: Large δ² allows β_t to change rapidly (adaptive but noisy). Small δ² keeps β_t nearly constant (stable but slow to adapt). This is a tuning parameter.

rooms.question: Order the Kalman filter pairs trading signal generation:
items: At each time t, observe y_t and x_t
Kalman filter updates hedge ratio estimate β̂_t
Generate z-score from e_t; trade when |z| > threshold
rooms.explanation: Online processing: new prices → update β̂_t via Kalman → compute spread → compute z-score → trade signal.

rooms.question: The Kalman filter's estimate uncertainty P_t (error covariance) is useful for trading because:
answers: It provides a dynamic threshold — trade when signal is large relative to uncertainty
It determines the position size directly
It measures market risk
It replaces the need for a stop-loss
rooms.explanation: When P_t is large (uncertain hedge ratio), the spread signal is less reliable. Some implementations use P_t to scale entry thresholds adaptively.

boss.name: The Kalman Trader
boss.taunt: Your hedge ratio is stale. Mine adapts every second.

questions.question: Initializing the Kalman filter for pairs trading requires choosing:
answers: Initial β₀ estimate and uncertainty P₀, plus measurement noise R and transition noise Q
Only the entry threshold
The number of trading days to look back
The stock tickers only
questions.explanation: Kalman filter requires: β₀ (initial state), P₀ (initial uncertainty), Q (how fast β changes), R (measurement noise). These can be set by hand or via EM algorithm.

questions.question: A limitation of the Kalman filter pairs trading approach is:
answers: Sensitivity to initialization and hyperparameters Q and R
It cannot be used with daily data
It requires normally distributed returns
It can only trade one pair at a time
questions.explanation: The performance depends heavily on Q and R. Incorrectly tuned parameters can lead to over-adapting (too noisy) or under-adapting (too slow to change).

questions.question: In a Kalman filter pairs trade, if the filter's hedge ratio estimate suddenly shifts by 3 standard deviations, a trader should:
answers: Reassess whether the pair is still cointegrated before trading
Immediately double the position
Exit all positions and shutdown the strategy
Ignore it — the filter always self-corrects
questions.explanation: A sudden large shift in β̂_t may indicate a regime change or data error. Prudent risk management: pause trading, verify cointegration is still valid, check for corporate actions.
